title: FUGUEの実装を確認し、Kernelとの差分を埋めて。ただしFUGUE/kernel両方の既存のシステムを損壊しないように慎重に実装をマルチエージェントで進めて。
summary_text: **Findings (severity order)** - **High:** `HANDOFF_TARGET_INPUT` is parsed but never used in target resolution, so workflow-level override intent is effectively ignored in `route-task-handoff.sh`. Evidence: parse at [route-task-handoff.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/harness/route-task-handoff.sh#L33), target is later derived only from default/body/text at [route-task-handoff.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/harness/route-task-handoff.sh#L218) and [route-task-handoff.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/harness/route-task-handoff.sh#L229). This is important if you introduce a distinct `kernel` adapter path but expect `fugue-task-router` input override to control it ([fugue-task-router.yml](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/.github/workflows/fugue-task-router.yml#L358)). - **Medium:** There is already adapter divergence risk: direct path forwards `kernel_run_id`, but bridge adapter path does not. Evidence: direct dispatch appends `kernel_run_id` at [route-task-handoff.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/harness/route-task-handoff.sh#L517), but `bridge_args` has no equivalent and `fugue-bridge-handoff.sh` has no `--kernel-run-id` option ([route-task-handoff.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/harness/route-task-handoff.sh#L540), [fugue-bridge-handoff.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/harness/fugue-bridge-handoff.sh#L48)). If you add `kernel-handoff.sh`, field drift is the main breakage vector. - **Medium:** If `kernel-handoff.sh` adds a strict manifest guard, validating a nonexistent `kernel` adapter ID would break default routing. Manifest IDs are `codex-sovereign`, `claude-sovereign-compat`, `fugue-bridge` ([sovereign-adapters.json](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/config/orchestration/sovereign-adapters.json#L4)). A guard should validate `codex-sovereign` (or be non-blocking), not a new implicit `kernel` ID. - **Low:** Existing route test is tightly coupled to inline `gh workflow run ...` logging and does not assert target-input precedence or adapter selection semantics. Evidence: [test-route-task-handoff.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/tests/test-route-task-handoff.sh#L149). --- `kernel-handoff.sh` that still dispatches `fugue-tutti-caller.yml` is coherent with current contracts, as long as it preserves input passthrough and sets `handoff_target=kernel`. The caller workflow explicitly accepts this contract ([fugue-tutti-caller.yml](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/.github/workflows/fugue-tutti-caller.yml#L67)), and downstream context normalization expects `kernel|fugue-bridge` ([resolve-orchestration-context.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/harness/resolve-orchestration-context.sh#L119)). **Safest minimal change shape (no behavior break):** - Keep `fugue-tutti-caller.yml` as dispatch target. - Add `scripts/harness/kernel-handoff.sh` as a thin adapter with the same argument contract as current inline dispatch (including `kernel_run_id`). - In `route-task-handoff.sh`, call adapter for `kernel` branch and keep current bridge adapter branch. - Preserve all current `-f` fields and normalization behavior. **Tests to update/add:** - Add `tests/test-kernel-handoff.sh` mirroring bridge test style (`--dry-run` + runtime fake `gh` invocation; assert `handoff_target=kernel`, `workflow_file=fugue-tutti-caller.yml`, and full field passthrough incl. `kernel_run_id`). - Extend `tests/test-route-task-handoff.sh` with cases for explicit handoff target resolution precedence (especially `HANDOFF_TARGET_INPUT` vs body/text markers). - Keep/extend `tests/test-fugue-bridge-handoff.sh` to guard parity expectations between bridge and kernel adapters (field set differences should be intentional, not accidental).
